title: buxus wrightii subsp. wrightii
intro: Buxus wrightii subsp. wrightii, also known as Wright's boxwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Buxaceae. It is a type of shrub or small tree that is native to the Philippines and is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage, offering health benefits such as relief from arthritis and protection against certain diseases. Buxus wrightii subsp. wrightii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
